Earth’s Ancient Past

The Paleozoic Era

Long before dinosaurs, life exploded in oceans, crawled onto land, and reshaped the planet forever. Explore the six periods that started it all.

Explore the Periods View Timeline

The Six Paleozoic Periods

Each period marks a major shift in life, climate, and Earth’s surface.
Click a card to learn what made each one unique.

541–485 Million Years Ago

Cambrian

The Cambrian Explosion introduced complex life in Earth’s oceans.

Learn more →
485–444 Million Years Ago

Ordovician

Marine life diversified, and the first plants began colonizing land.

Learn more →
444–419 Million Years Ago

Silurian

Life moved onto land with early plants and jawed fish.

Learn more →
419–359 Million Years Ago

Devonian

Known as the “Age of Fishes,” forests spread across the land.

Learn more →
359–299 Million Years Ago

Carboniferous

Giant insects and swampy forests that formed today’s coal.

Learn more →
299–252 Million Years Ago

Permian

The final Paleozoic chapter ends with Earth’s largest extinction.

Learn more →
